findings.title: Conflict-accepted green tests do not prove the required successful checks
findings.description: With PANOP_LLM_PROBE_ACCEPT_CONFLICT=1, the live P-LLM test stops requiring verdict.green and then only checks ticket id, budget constants, attempts length, traceSecretClean, noRawKeyRoute, and that at least one blocker mentions latency, no-host, or cost. It does not assert deterministic, mappedActionToolSchema, actPromptAmendment, or a successful subscription-routed candidate. The paired A-LLM-SUB test likewise accepts any non-green binding-conflict summary without requiring a passed host subscription attempt. That means the recorded green logs could pass even if the model was unreachable or the required decision/schema/prompt checks failed, so the tests are weaker than the ticket mandate.

findings.title: Trace-secret verdict is computed before the full probe trace exists
findings.description: runHotLoopSubscriptionProbe sets checks.traceSecretClean before writing the cached verdict and before appending the llm_probe.verdict trace line. The test then asserts that stale boolean, so it does not prove that the complete trace, including the final verdict event, contains zero key-shaped strings. A later artifact scan may catch current output, but the probe assertion itself does not cover the full trace it creates.
